non_claims: does not identify the source scalar with H_unit by definition
does not use observed top mass or observed Yukawa as input
does not promote the Ward theorem
proposal_allowed_reason: The runner proves a missing normalization condition, not retained top-Yukawa closure.
required_new_theorem: derive the scalar source two-point residue and prove that the canonical Higgs-doublet normalization gives kappa_H = 1, or carry the measured/derived kappa_H as a separate physical input
verdict: The current source/count/SSB data do not derive kappa_H = 1.  Different kappa_H values preserve the same group-count coefficient and the same SSB identity while producing different doublet Yukawa readouts.  A scalar two-point residue or equivalent LSZ theorem is required to select kappa_H.
